perturbation.original_text: Developer represents that it is experienced in and has independent knowledge of the nature and specifics of the restaurant business. Developer represents that in entering into this Agreement it has relied solely on its personal knowledge and has not relied on any representations of Franchisor or any of its officers, directors, employees or agents, except those representations contained in any legally required Franchise Disclosure Document delivered to Developer.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer represents that it is experienced in and has independent knowledge of the nature and specifics of the restaurant business. Developer acknowledges that in entering into this Agreement it has relied solely on the representations of Franchisor or any of its officers, directors, employees or agents, except those representations contained in any legally required Franchise Disclosure Document delivered to Developer.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'has not relied' to 'has relied' could be interpreted as a waiver of rights related to misrepresentation, going against franchise laws designed to protect franchisees. This introduces ambiguity about the franchisee's reliance on the franchisor's claims, potentially undermining the protections offered by franchise disclosure laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification contradicts the spirit of the California Franchise Relations Act, which aims to protect franchisees from deceptive practices by ensuring they can rely on accurate information. By stating the franchisee *has* relied on franchisor representations, it could weaken their ability to claim damages for misrepresentation later, undermining the law's intent.

SPECIAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [18400 - 22949.92.2] ( Division 8 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 44. ) CHAPTER 5.5. Franchise Relations [20000 - 20044] ( Chapter 5.5 added by Stats. 1980, Ch. 1355, Sec. 1. ) ARTICLE 1. Definitions [20000 - 20010] ( Article 1 added by Stats. 1980, Ch. 1355, Sec. 1. ) 20010. Any condition, stipulation or provision purporting to bind any person to waive compliance with any provision of this law is contrary to public policy and void. (Added by Stats. 1980, Ch. 1355, Sec. 1. Effective October 1, 1980. Operative January 1, 1981, by Sec. 6 of Ch. 1355.)
perturbation.justification: The change from 'has not relied' to 'has relied' directly impacts the franchisee's ability to claim misrepresentation, undermining the protective intent of franchise laws. Snippet 1, BPC 20010, states that 'Any condition, stipulation or provision purporting to bind any person to waive compliance with any provision of this law is contrary to public policy and void.' The modified text could be interpreted as the franchisee waiving their right to claim reliance on the franchisor's representations, thus potentially waiving rights under franchise law designed to protect them from deceptive practices. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction' definition, where the contractual language deviates from legally recognized definitions, making the contract unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Developer also shall carry such worker's compensation insurance as may be required by applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer may, at its discretion, carry worker's compensation insurance.
perturbation.explanation: Making worker's compensation optional directly contradicts mandatory worker's compensation laws. This shift introduces uncertainty about compliance with state labor regulations and potentially exposes the franchisor to liability.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Labor Code Section 3700 mandates that all employers secure the payment of workers' compensation. Making it discretionary directly violates this legal requirement, exposing the developer and potentially the franchisor to significant penalties and liabilities.

WORKERS' COMPENSATION AND INSURANCE [3200 - 6002] ( Heading of Division 4 amended by Stats. 1979, Ch. 373. ) PART 1. SCOPE AND OPERATION [3200 - 4418] ( Part 1 enacted by Stats. 1937, Ch. 90. ) CHAPTER 4. Compensation Insurance and Security [3700 - 3823] ( Chapter 4 enacted by Stats. 1937, Ch. 90. ) ARTICLE 1. Insurance and Security [3700 - 3709.5] ( Article 1 enacted by Stats. 1937, Ch. 90. ) 3700. Every employer except the state shall secure the payment of compensation in one or more of the following ways: (a) By being insured against liability to pay compensation by one or more insurers duly authorized to write compensation insurance in this state. (b) By securing from the Director of Industrial Relations a certificate of consent to self-insure either as an individual employer, or as one employer in a group of employers, which may be given upon furnishing proof satisfactory to the Director of Industrial Relations of ability to self-insure and to pay any compensation that may become due to his or her employees. (c) For any county, city, city and county, municipal corporation, public district, public agency, or any political subdivision of the state, including each member of a pooling arrangement under a joint exercise of powers agreement (but not
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes a mandatory requirement for worker's compensation insurance to a discretionary one. California Labor Code Section 3700, as quoted in Scraped Law Snippet 1, states that 'Every employer except the state shall secure the payment of compensation...' This clearly mandates that employers must secure worker's compensation, contradicting the changed text that allows the developer to 'may, at its discretion, carry worker's compensation insurance.'

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement, after review by Developer and El Pollo Loco, was accepted in the state in which Franchisor's then-current headquarters (currently the State of California) is located and shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of such state, except that the provisions in Section 20.1 covering competition following the expiration, termination or assignment of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the state in which the breach occurs.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of [Developer's State of Residence], without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies California law governs the contract, but the modified version states that it will follow the developer's residential state laws. This undermines the predictability of contract interpretation and introduces potential conflicts of laws issues. This introduces ambiguity regarding applicable law, potentially leading to disputes over which state's laws govern the contract's interpretation and enforcement, contradicting the principle of clear choice of law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law - Choice of Law Principles
perturbation.law_explanation: While parties generally have the freedom to choose the governing law, this modification introduces uncertainty and contradicts the original intent. The Restatement (Second) of Conflict of Laws § 187 emphasizes the importance of a clear contractual choice of law. The developer's resident state law can affect the enforceability of the existing contract depending on that state's laws.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: conflict of laws | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you conflict of laws Conflict of laws refers to a difference between the laws of two or more jurisdictions with some connection to a case, such that the outcome depends on which jurisdiction's law will be used to resolve each issue in dispute . The conflicting legal rules may come from U.S. federal law, the laws of U.S. states, or the laws of other countries. The process by which a court determines what law to apply is sometimes referred to as "characterization" or "classification." This determination must be made in accordance with the law of the forum . A federal court in a case before it based on diversity of citizenship , for example, determines the conflict of law issue as if it were the highest court in the state in which it is sitting. Courts faced with a choice of law issue generally have two choices: A court can apply the law of the forum ( lex fori ) -- which is usually the result when the question of what law to apply is procedural . Or the court can apply the law of the site of the transaction or occurrence that gave rise to the litigation in the first place ( lex loci ) -- this is usually the controlling law selected when the matter is substantive . Federal courts play by different rules than state courts because federal jurisdiction is limited to what has been enumerated in the Constitution . The rules that federal courts must obey regarding which laws to apply are extremely complex. perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original contract's choice of law provision. The original text specifies California law, while the modified text substitutes the law of the 'Developer's State of Residence'. This creates a conflict of laws issue, as highlighted by Scraped Law Snippet 1: 'conflict of laws refers to a difference between the laws of two or more jurisdictions with some connection to a case, such that the outcome depends on which jurisdiction's law will be used to resolve each issue in dispute'. The change introduces ambiguity and uncertainty about which state's laws will govern the contract, conflicting with the principle of a clear choice of law.
